content: We can reframe the task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a meta-architecture for instantiating multi-perspective LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where each iterative self-feedback round pulls a new inconsistent problem-solving perspective from the LLM’s own parametric knowledge to refine the initial output. This eliminates the need to hand-engineer distinct reflection perspectives or add extra model parameters, leveraging the LLM’s inherent ability to generate varied outputs to power more robust reflection.
